endangeredFish.description: The only freshwater sardine in the world, endemic to Taal Lake in Batangas. This unique species adapted to freshwater from marine ancestors.
endangeredFish.habitat: Freshwater lake (Taal Lake, Batangas)
endangeredFish.distribution: Endemic to Taal Lake, Philippines - the only place in the world where this species exists
endangeredFish.threats: Overfishing, pollution from aquaculture, introduction of invasive species, habitat degradation, volcanic activity
endangeredFish.importance: Unique evolutionary adaptation, only freshwater sardine species globally, important for local fishing communities, indicator of lake ecosystem health
endangeredFish.conservationEfforts: Fishing regulations and closed seasons, lake rehabilitation programs, monitoring of fish populations, community-based conservation initiatives

endangeredFish.description: The world's smallest commercial fish, measuring only 1.2-1.4 cm. Endemic to Lake Buhi in Camarines Sur. Known locally as 'tabios'.
endangeredFish.habitat: Freshwater lake (Lake Buhi, Camarines Sur)
endangeredFish.distribution: Endemic to Lake Buhi and nearby lakes in Bicol Region, Philippines
endangeredFish.threats: Habitat destruction, water pollution, introduction of predatory fish species, siltation, climate change affecting water levels
endangeredFish.importance: World's smallest commercial fish, unique Philippine endemic species, important for local economy and culture, indicator species for lake health
endangeredFish.conservationEfforts: Lake protection programs, fishing regulations, habitat restoration, community education, research on breeding and conservation

endangeredFish.description: Also known as 'Mameng' in the Philippines. Large, colorful reef fish that can grow over 2 meters. Highly valued in live fish trade.
endangeredFish.habitat: Coral reefs in tropical waters, lagoons, and outer reef slopes
endangeredFish.distribution: Indo-Pacific region including Philippines, Indonesia, Malaysia, Australia, and Red Sea
endangeredFish.threats: Overfishing for live fish trade (especially to Hong Kong and China), habitat destruction, cyanide fishing, slow reproductive rate
endangeredFish.importance: Apex predator on coral reefs, maintains reef ecosystem balance, important for reef health and biodiversity
endangeredFish.conservationEfforts: Marine protected areas, fishing bans in some areas, CITES listing, size and catch limits, reef conservation programs

endangeredFish.description: Also known as 'Lapu-lapu' in the Philippines. One of the largest reef fish, can grow over 2.5 meters and weigh 400 kg.
endangeredFish.habitat: Coral reefs, rocky areas, and lagoons in tropical and subtropical waters
endangeredFish.distribution: Indo-Pacific region including Philippines, from Red Sea to Australia and Japan
endangeredFish.threats: Overfishing for live fish trade, habitat destruction of coral reefs, slow growth and late maturity, bycatch
endangeredFish.importance: Top predator on coral reefs, maintains reef ecosystem balance, culturally significant in Philippines, important for reef health
endangeredFish.conservationEfforts: Marine protected areas, size and catch limits, reef conservation programs, aquaculture research, fishing regulations

endangeredFish.description: Endemic freshwater goby species found in Philippine rivers and lakes. Important for local fisheries and ecosystem balance.
endangeredFish.habitat: Freshwater rivers, streams, and lakes throughout the Philippines
endangeredFish.distribution: Endemic to freshwater systems in the Philippines
endangeredFish.threats: Habitat destruction from dam construction, water pollution, overfishing, introduction of invasive species, deforestation affecting water quality
endangeredFish.importance: Important food fish for local communities, indicator of freshwater ecosystem health, part of traditional Filipino diet
endangeredFish.conservationEfforts: River rehabilitation programs, fishing regulations, habitat protection, water quality monitoring, community-based conservation

endangeredFish.description: Beautiful spotted grouper found in Philippine coral reefs. Highly valued in the live fish trade and local markets.
endangeredFish.habitat: Coral reefs, particularly outer reef slopes and channels
endangeredFish.distribution: Indo-Pacific region including Philippines, from East Africa to Pacific islands
endangeredFish.threats: Overfishing for live fish trade, habitat destruction, cyanide fishing, coral reef degradation
endangeredFish.importance: Important reef predator, maintains fish population balance, valuable commercial species, indicator of reef health
endangeredFish.conservationEfforts: Marine protected areas, fishing regulations, reef conservation, size limits, seasonal closures

endangeredFish.description: Also known as Milkfish. While not globally endangered, wild populations in the Philippines face threats from habitat loss and overfishing.
endangeredFish.habitat: Coastal waters, lagoons, and estuaries. Spawns in open sea, juveniles migrate to shallow waters
endangeredFish.distribution: Indo-Pacific region, particularly important in Philippines, Indonesia, and Taiwan
endangeredFish.threats: Habitat destruction of spawning grounds, coastal development, pollution, overfishing of wild stocks, climate change
endangeredFish.importance: National fish of the Philippines, culturally and economically significant, important food source, supports aquaculture industry
endangeredFish.conservationEfforts: Marine protected areas for spawning grounds, sustainable fishing practices, aquaculture to reduce wild catch pressure, habitat protection

endangeredFish.description: Colorful reef fish that lives in symbiosis with sea anemones. Popular in aquarium trade, putting pressure on wild populations.
endangeredFish.habitat: Coral reefs with sea anemones, shallow lagoons and reef flats
endangeredFish.distribution: Indo-Pacific region including Philippines, Indonesia, and Australia
endangeredFish.threats: Collection for aquarium trade, habitat destruction of coral reefs, climate change affecting coral health, pollution
endangeredFish.importance: Important for reef ecosystem, popular in marine aquarium trade, indicator of reef health, symbiotic relationship with anemones
endangeredFish.conservationEfforts: Sustainable collection practices, captive breeding programs, marine protected areas, reef conservation

endangeredFish.description: Large, colorful angelfish found in Philippine coral reefs. Valued in aquarium trade and as food fish.
endangeredFish.habitat: Coral reefs, particularly outer reef slopes and drop-offs
endangeredFish.distribution: Indo-Pacific region including Philippines, from Red Sea to Pacific islands
endangeredFish.threats: Collection for aquarium trade, habitat destruction, overfishing, coral reef degradation
endangeredFish.importance: Important reef species, maintains reef ecosystem balance, valuable in aquarium trade, indicator of reef health
endangeredFish.conservationEfforts: Marine protected areas, sustainable collection practices, reef conservation, size and catch limits

endangeredFish.description: Colorful reef fish that plays crucial role in maintaining coral reef health by eating algae. Multiple species found in Philippines.
endangeredFish.habitat: Coral reefs, seagrass beds, and rocky areas
endangeredFish.distribution: Tropical and subtropical waters worldwide, abundant in Philippines
endangeredFish.threats: Overfishing, habitat destruction of coral reefs, pollution, climate change affecting coral health
endangeredFish.importance: Critical for reef health (controls algae growth), important for coral reef ecosystem balance, supports reef biodiversity
endangeredFish.conservationEfforts: Marine protected areas, fishing regulations, reef conservation programs, protection of key habitats

endangeredFish.description: Cleaner wrasse that removes parasites from other fish. Important for reef ecosystem health and fish health maintenance.
endangeredFish.habitat: Coral reefs, particularly areas with cleaning stations
endangeredFish.distribution: Indo-Pacific region including Philippines, from Red Sea to Pacific islands
endangeredFish.threats: Collection for aquarium trade, habitat destruction, coral reef degradation, pollution
endangeredFish.importance: Essential for reef fish health (removes parasites), maintains reef ecosystem balance, important symbiotic relationships
endangeredFish.conservationEfforts: Marine protected areas, sustainable collection practices, reef conservation, protection of cleaning stations

endangeredFish.description: Large, aggressive reef fish with distinctive patterns. Important reef predator that helps maintain ecosystem balance.

endangeredFish.distribution: Indo-Pacific region including Philippines, from East Africa to Pacific islands

endangeredFish.importance: Important reef predator, maintains ecosystem balance, controls sea urchin populations, indicator of reef health

endangeredFish.description: Herbivorous reef fish with sharp spines. Multiple species found in Philippines, important for controlling algae on reefs.

endangeredFish.distribution: Tropical and subtropical waters worldwide, abundant in Philippines

endangeredFish.description: Colorful reef fish with distinctive patterns. Found in Philippine coral reefs, valued in local fisheries.

endangeredFish.distribution: Indo-Pacific region including Philippines, from East Africa to Pacific islands

endangeredFish.distribution: Tropical and subtropical waters worldwide, abundant in Philippines
